findingDetails.title: Delta validation does not enforce canonical-record parity
findingDetails.evidence: Only numeric iteration identity is compared; delta v2 content is not independently validated before reducer consumption.
findingDetails.recommendation: Enforce canonical field parity or independently validate the delta iteration record.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact validator/test search found only route-proof and iteration-id checks; deep-review reducer loads delta payloads.

claimAdjudication.claim: A contradictory delta canonical record can pass gates and reach reducer input.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched validators and tests for canonical field equality or independent delta validation.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Per-event rows are independent, but the canonical iteration row is contractually shared.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: A mandatory downstream pre-reducer parity gate is demonstrated.

findingDetails.title: Narrative verdict gate accepts a non-final or duplicated verdict
findingDetails.evidence: The unanchored regex accepts any verdict occurrence despite the absolute-final-line and uniqueness contract.
findingDetails.recommendation: Require one exact case-sensitive verdict as the trimmed final line.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search found the owning contract and sole runtime verifier, with no final-line or uniqueness test.

claimAdjudication.claim: The mechanical gate accepts narratives violating the mandatory verdict contract.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched validators, workflow caller, and tests for final-line or uniqueness enforcement.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Verdict may be re-derived downstream, but malformed artifact acceptance still violates this gate.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: A mandatory earlier gate rejecting non-final or duplicate verdicts is demonstrated.

findingDetails.title: Dispatch receipts cannot authenticate across the workflow process boundary
findingDetails.evidence: The random module-scoped signing secret exists only in the standalone dispatch process; fresh-process verification of the live iteration-1 receipt returned false.
findingDetails.recommendation: Share verification authority through a trusted process boundary and add a cross-process end-to-end test.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Workflow wiring, tests, and live receipt replay show no key handoff across processes.

claimAdjudication.claim: An authentic dispatch receipt produced by the auto workflow cannot be verified by a fresh post-dispatch validator process.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Inspected workflow wiring and receipt tests and replayed the live receipt from a fresh process; no key handoff exists.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Validation could share the producer process, but the YAML dispatch command exits first.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Production proves one module instance spans dispatch and validation or identifies protected key handoff.

findingDetails.title: The advertised permissions gate is not connected to executor dispatch
findingDetails.evidence: Only definitions and tests call the gate; active OpenCode dispatch bypasses permissions and has no trusted workspace mutation reconciliation.
findingDetails.recommendation: Wire enforcement to dispatch/tool execution and add before/after allowed-write verification.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact repository search found no production evaluator caller or post-dispatch mutation gate.

claimAdjudication.claim: Deep-review executor dispatch has no runtime-enforced packet write boundary despite cataloguing a shipped pre-dispatch permissions gate.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched all .opencode code and YAML for evaluator calls and runtime workspace mutation checks; only definitions and tests were found.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: An external orchestrator may eventually call the library, but the active workflow does not and the catalog says shipped.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: A production caller is shown to intercept every executor tool call or a trusted mutation gate rejects out-of-packet writes.

results.summary: Receipt authority and write-boundary claims were checked against active workflow wiring.
F009 is catalogued as shipped, but no production dispatch caller exists.

findingDetails.title: Cross-runtime LEAF contracts omit the required delta artifact and contradict append-only state handling

findingDetails.recommendation: Align both runtime agents and the playbook with the workflow's append-only narrative/state/delta contract.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search confirmed identical stale instructions in both runtime agents and contradictory artifact identities in the playbook.

claimAdjudication.claim: Canonical LEAF instructions conflict with the active workflow's required artifact and append-only state contract.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched both runtime agents, owning skill, active workflow, playbook, and stress test for delta authorization and append/overwrite semantics.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: The agent text may predate LEAF-owned delta generation, but the active dispatch contract currently requires that generation before validation.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Reducer-owned delta generation plus proof that the overwrite step is unreachable, or complete contract alignment.

findingDetails.title: The canonical review-mode render contract has no executable renderer
findingDetails.evidence: Six render targets and validation checks are declared, but no renderer, populated target markers, or generated snapshot exists.
findingDetails.recommendation: Implement and gate one verify-capable renderer or remove the unimplemented generation claims.

claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: The manifest may be aspirational and consumers may be intentionally manual.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: An executable verify-capable renderer and generated targets are identified.

findingDetails.title: Runtime command injection consumes stale compiled contracts without checking source freshness
findingDetails.evidence: The renderer injects the compiled file directly; digest replay and the focused drift test show the checked-in review contract is stale.
findingDetails.recommendation: Gate compilation freshness and reject or regenerate stale contracts before injection.

claimAdjudication.claim: The runtime command renderer injects checked-in compiled contracts without source-digest validation, and the current review contract is stale.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Read compiler, renderer, and tests; replayed source hashes and ran the focused drift suite.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: CI may be intended as the only freshness boundary and the tree may be between edit and regeneration.

findingDetails.title: Checked incident claims lack durable evidence and conflict with the active retry lineage
findingDetails.evidence: CHK-010, CHK-020, and CHK-021 are checked P0 claims without durable evidence references; packet continuity says rerun approval is pending while current config/state prove a fresh lineage at iteration 5.
findingDetails.recommendation: Attach durable evidence or reopen the checks, then reconcile packet continuity with the retry lineage.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All seven checked items were inspected; three P0 checks rely only on historical prose.

claimAdjudication.claim: Three checked P0 incident claims lack durable evidence references and the checklist's pending-rerun continuity contradicts the active retry lineage at iteration 5.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Inspected every checked checklist item, its named task and implementation-summary support, the current review config, and the current state log for durable evidence or a lineage qualifier.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: The checklist may intentionally describe only the destroyed predecessor run, but its unqualified continuity fields still present that state as current and the checked P0 evidence remains non-durable.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if durable evidence substantiates the three checks and predecessor continuity is explicitly separated from the active retry.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: The packet contains multiple checked P0/P1 claims and an active cross-artifact retry lineage.

claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: The documents may predate delta rollout, but that still leaves the current dispatched LEAF unable to satisfy the validator.

focus: correctness second pass: deep-ai-council and deep-improvement mode packets

findingDetails.title: Council convergence discards proposal identity before stability scoring
findingDetails.evidence: The production parser reduces every seat plan to a generic support stance; default merge and stability scoring can therefore stop on two materially different supported plans.
findingDetails.recommendation: Carry a material plan identifier through seat verdicts and test conflicting supported plans.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Prompt, parser, default adjudicator, scorer, and CLI tests were directly reviewed.

claimAdjudication.claim: The default deep-ai-council session path can declare verdict stability when generic seat stances remain SUPPORT even though the supported plans materially differ.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked custom adjudication and production tests for plan-identity preservation.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Custom adjudication can preserve identity but is absent from the normal CLI path.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Production always injects a material-plan adjudicator or default merge is proven unreachable.

findingDetails.title: Auto model-benchmark synthesis executes an unbound promotion step
findingDetails.evidence: Auto inputs define neither candidate_path nor target_path, but unconditional synthesis invokes promotion with both and --approve; the helper rejects missing arguments.
findingDetails.recommendation: Remove auto promotion or add explicit bindings and a real approval condition.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Auto/confirm workflows, router semantics, and helper arguments were directly reviewed.

claimAdjudication.claim: The Lane B auto workflow reaches a promotion command whose required candidate and target placeholders are not defined by that workflow.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched workflow assets and router/helper contracts for bindings or skip semantics.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: An AI may interpret 'Offer' as skip, but no executable condition encodes that behavior.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: A verified executor rule and end-to-end trace prove the command is skipped before placeholder evaluation.

searchActions.queryOrPath: seat parser -> default merge -> verdict scorer

findingDetails.title: Model-benchmark workflow interpolates an unvalidated run label into shell commands
findingDetails.evidence: Both workflows place run_label in shell commands before any sanitizer; declared validation omits run_label and run-benchmark sanitizeLabel only protects a later history filename.
findingDetails.recommendation: Validate run_label with a strict basename allowlist before rendering and quote every derived path.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search covered both workflows and model-benchmark scripts; no pre-dispatch run-label sanitizer was found.

claimAdjudication.claim: The model-benchmark workflows can interpret a profile- or command-derived run label as shell syntax before the benchmark runner's filename sanitizer executes.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched both workflows and model-benchmark scripts for validation, slugging, sanitization, and shell quoting.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: The orchestration layer may derive run_label only from trusted profiles, but that invariant is not declared or enforced.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the renderer rejects shell metacharacters before command execution with an executable auto/confirm regression test.

focus: maintainability second pass: deep-ai-council, deep-improvement, deep-research, and manual-testing-playbook modules

findingDetails.title: Grader cache identity omits the effective model and mutable prompt content
findingDetails.evidence: The cache key uses an optional caller placeholder plus prompt pathname while real dispatch selects GRADER_MODEL dynamically and reads mutable prompt bytes.
findingDetails.recommendation: Key cache entries from the effective model build identity and selected prompt-content hash, or disable reuse when identity is unavailable.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search covered the cache producer and all discovered gradeD4/buildGraderBase consumers; none supplies both identities.

claimAdjudication.claim: The grader cache can reuse a stale score after the effective grader model or system-prompt contents change because neither value is represented by content identity in the cache key.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched all discovered model-benchmark CJS consumers for model-build and prompt-content identity plumbing; none supplies both.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Unique immutable run-scoped cache roots would contain the issue, but the harness permits a shared fallback and does not enforce immutability.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Prove all production callers use unique immutable cache roots and reject model or prompt changes during each root lifetime.

results.summary: Declared grader-build cache identity is not bound to the effective model or prompt contents.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Cross-consumer cache identity spans benchmark dispatch, mutable configuration, cache storage, and multiple scoring callers.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Broad maintainability search identified an explicit placeholder at a score-reproducibility boundary; producer-consumer tracing selected every discovered caller.

searchLedger.invariant: A cached model-derived score must be keyed by every mutable input that can alter the score.

searchActions.result: All discovered callers omit effective model and prompt-content identity.
Shared fallback and placeholder identity permit stale cache reuse.

searchLedger.rationale: The effective model and prompt bytes are mutable scoring inputs absent from the key.

focus: final sweep: benchmark and changelog submodules plus checklist_evidence closure

findingDetails.title: Live benchmark reports PASS while presenting Mode-A-only and P1 evidence as complete
findingDetails.evidence: The live report carries Mode-A-only unscored labels and rerun guidance while PASS is computed before a P1 bottleneck is added.
findingDetails.recommendation: Make unscored reasons trace-mode-aware and prevent PASS when active P1 bottlenecks or required unscored dimensions remain.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared all four JSON/Markdown report pairs and traced labels and verdict generation to scorer and renderer.

claimAdjudication.claim: The live benchmark report presents an incomplete run as PASS while emitting Mode-A-only rerun guidance and an active P1 funnel bottleneck.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Compared all report pairs, README caveats, trace metadata, verdict rules, and bottleneck rendering for an explicit narrower PASS contract.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: PASS may mean aggregate-only and extra flags may be required, but the report does not communicate that meaning and labels its own failures P1.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Explicitly constrain PASS to aggregate-only, prove consumers cannot treat it as completion, and correct the live-run wording.

findingDetails.title: Reducer preserves resolved checklist search debt and drops the executed failure from current traceability
findingDetails.evidence: Iteration 5 executed and failed checklist_evidence, but current reduced state keeps it deferred and latest-wins traceability omits it.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile later covered findings against earlier search debt and merge cumulative protocol results.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Traced original deferral, iteration-5 closure, current registry, and both reducer aggregation paths; no later re-deferral exists.

claimAdjudication.claim: Canonical reduced state still treats checklist_evidence as deferred and omits its executed failure even though iteration 5 closed the obligation with an evidenced P1 finding.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched later iteration records, registry fields, strategy status, and reducer reconciliation for renewed deferral or debt-clearing behavior.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Historical flags may be intentional, but they leave resolved debt active and canonical current traceability incomplete.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Prove legal STOP and all consumers ignore searchDebt and latest traceability and derive completion directly from immutable iteration history.

results.summary: Live benchmark claims conflict with scorer and report evidence.
Protocol was executed and failed in iteration 5; reducer state still misclassifies it as deferred.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Final sweep spans generated benchmark evidence, scorer/renderer producers, changelog history, and cross-iteration reducer state.

searchActions.result: Only live report combines live metadata with Mode-A rerun labels and PASS plus P1.

note: Hard ceiling reached (iteration_count 10 >= max_iterations 10). This is an excluded reason for the divergent-mode pivot eligibility contract (eligible_reasons=[all_dimensions_clean] only; maxIterationsReached is explicitly excluded), so no Council pivot is triggered. Legal-stop gates were not satisfied at this checkpoint (graph_decision=STOP_BLOCKED, blockers=uncovered_dimensions+unstable_findings) and are preserved here as terminal evidence per the hard-stop algorithm (step 1a: proceed to synthesis without legal-stop veto). Loop hands off to phase_synthesis next.
